sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) pr. that which is before, adv. before, Psa 139:5. Hence-

(2) the east, the eastern quarter (comp. אָחֹור), Job 23:8. מִקֶּדֶם from the east; Gen 2:8 12:8 . לְ מִקֶּדֶם prep. eastward of any place, Gen 3:24 Num 34:11 Jos 7:2 Jud. 8:11 . בְּנֵי קֶדֶם “the sons of the east,” are the inhabitants of Arabia Deserta, which stretches eastward of Palestine to the Euphrates; now called بدية الشام the desert of Syria, Job 1:3 Isa 11:14 Jer 49:28 Eze 25:4 1Ki 5:10 Jud. 6:3 , seqq.; hence אֶרֶץ קֶדֶם Gen 25:6, and אֶרֶץ בְּנֵי קֶדֶם Gen 29:1, of Arabia Deserta; הַר־הַקֶּדֶם the mountains of Arabia, Gen 10:30 (see under the word מֵשָׁא ). Sometimes קֶדֶם also includes Mesopotamia and Babylonia, Numb. 23:7 , and Isa 2:6, מָֽלְאוּ מִקֶּדֶם “they are full of the east,” i.e. of superstitions and sorceries brought from the east or from Babylonia.

(3) used of time, ancient time, poet. i.q. עֹולָם No. 1. (Arab. قِدْمُ ancient time, قِْدمًا of old, anciently). Psa 44:2 Isai. 23:7 . מִקֶּדֶם from of old, anciently, Psa 74:12 77:6 , 12 Psa 77:12. מַלְכֵי קֶדֶם ancient kings, Isai. 19:11 יְמֵי קֶדֶם ancient times, Psa 44:2. Used even of eternity, of that at least which has no beginning, e.g. אֱלֹהֵי קֶדֶם Deu 33:27 ישֵׁב קֶדֶם he who sits on the throne from eternity, Psa 55:20. It is used also-(a) adverbially for, of old, Jer 30:20 Lam 5:21 i.q. לְפָנִים, מִלְּפָנִים.-(b) it becomes a prep. before, Pro 8:22. const. קַדְמֵי beginnings, Pro 8:23.
